"""
WiLI-2018 Language Detection Benchmark
Dataset: /kaggle/input/datasets/mexwell/wili-2018
Tests top-performing + fast models from prior basilb2s benchmark.
"""

import time
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, accuracy_score

# Models (top + fast only)
from sklearn.linear_model import SGDClassifier, RidgeClassifier, PassiveAggressiveClassifier
from sklearn.svm import LinearSVC
from sklearn.naive_bayes import ComplementNB
from sklearn.neighbors import NearestCentroid
from sklearn.neural_network import MLPClassifier


# ── 1. Load WiLI-2018 ────────────────────────────────────────────
DATA_DIR = "../dataset/raw/"

print("Loading WiLI-2018 dataset...")

with open(DATA_DIR + "x_train.txt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    X_train_raw = [line.strip() for line in f]

with open(DATA_DIR + "y_train.txt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    y_train_raw = [line.strip() for line in f]

with open(DATA_DIR + "x_test.txt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    X_test_raw = [line.strip() for line in f]

with open(DATA_DIR + "y_test.txt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    y_test_raw = [line.strip() for line in f]

print(f"  Train samples : {len(X_train_raw):,}")
print(f"  Test  samples : {len(X_test_raw):,}")
print(f"  Languages     : {len(set(y_train_raw))}")


# ── 2. Preprocessing ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Minimal cleaning: strip whitespace, drop empty lines
def clean(texts, labels):
    pairs = [(t.strip(), l) for t, l in zip(texts, labels) if t.strip()]
    texts_c, labels_c = zip(*pairs)
    return list(texts_c), list(labels_c)

X_train_raw, y_train_raw = clean(X_train_raw, y_train_raw)
X_test_raw,  y_test_raw  = clean(X_test_raw,  y_test_raw)

# Encode labels
le = LabelEncoder()
le.fit(y_train_raw + y_test_raw)          # fit on all so unseen test labels don't error
y_train = le.transform(y_train_raw)
y_test  = le.transform(y_test_raw)


# ── 3. Feature Extraction ────────────────────────────────────────
print("\nFitting TF-IDF vectorizer (char_wb 2-4 grams, 50k features)...")
t0 = time.time()

vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
    analyzer="char_wb",
    ngram_range=(2, 4),
    max_features=50_000,
    sublinear_tf=True,
)

X_train = vectorizer.fit_transform(X_train_raw)
X_test  = vectorizer.transform(X_test_raw)

print(f"  Done in {time.time() - t0:.1f}s | Matrix shape: {X_train.shape}")


# ── 4. Model Registry (top + fast) ──────────────────────────────
models = {
    # ~instant – strong baselines
    "Complement NB":          ComplementNB(),
    "SGD Classifier":         SGDClassifier(loss="modified_huber", class_weight="balanced",
                                            random_state=42, n_jobs=-1),
    "Passive Aggressive":     PassiveAggressiveClassifier(class_weight="balanced",
                                                          random_state=42, n_jobs=-1),
    # fast linear – top performers on basilb2s
    "Ridge Classifier":       RidgeClassifier(class_weight="balanced"),
    "Linear SVC":             LinearSVC(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42,
                                        max_iter=2000),
    # geometric centroid – surprisingly strong on WiLI-style data
    "Nearest Centroid":       NearestCentroid(),
    # heavier – only run if time permits (comment out to skip)
    "MLP Classifier":         MLPClassifier(hidden_layer_sizes=(256, 128),
                                            max_iter=300, random_state=42,
                                            early_stopping=True, n_iter_no_change=10),
}


# ── 5. Evaluation Loop ───────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n" + "─" * 72)
print(f"{'Model':<28} {'Macro F1':>9} {'Weighted F1':>12} {'Accuracy':>9} {'Time':>8}")
print("─" * 72)

results = []

for name, model in models.items():
    try:
        t0 = time.time()
        model.fit(X_train, y_train)
        y_pred = model.predict(X_test)
        elapsed = time.time() - t0

        macro_f1    = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average="macro",    zero_division=0)
        weighted_f1 = f1_score(y_test, y_pred, average="weighted", zero_division=0)
        accuracy    = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)

        print(f"✅ {name:<26} {macro_f1:>9.4f} {weighted_f1:>12.4f} {accuracy:>9.4f} {elapsed:>7.1f}s")
        results.append({
            "Model": name,
            "Macro F1": macro_f1,
            "Weighted F1": weighted_f1,
            "Accuracy": accuracy,
            "Time (s)": round(elapsed, 2),
        })
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"❌ {name}: {e}")

print("─" * 72)


# ── 6. Summary Table ────────────────────────────────────────────
results_df = (
    pd.DataFrame(results)
    .sort_values("Macro F1", ascending=False)
    .reset_index(drop=True)
)
results_df.index += 1   # 1-based rank

print("\n📊 Final Rankings (sorted by Macro F1):\n")
print(results_df.to_string())

# Optional: save to CSV
results_df.to_csv("wili_benchmark_results.csv", index_label="Rank")
print("\nResults saved to wili_benchmark_results.csv")

"""
WiLI-2018 — LangDetect-style Naive Bayes vs. google-langdetect library
========================================================================
Architecture mirrors langdetect (https://github.com/shuyo/language-detection):
  - Character n-gram profiles (1–3 grams)
  - TF-IDF weighting with sublinear_tf
  - Complement Naive Bayes (works better than Multinomial on imbalanced multilingual data)

Evaluation:
  - Our model  : trained on ALL WiLI languages, tested on langdetect-supported subset
  - langdetect : same test subset, tested via the library
"""

import re
import time
import warnings
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
from sklearn.naive_bayes import ComplementNB
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, accuracy_score, classification_report

warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

# ── langdetect supported languages (75 languages as of v1.2.3) ───
LANGDETECT_LANGS = {
    "af", "ar", "bg", "bn", "ca", "cs", "cy", "da", "de", "el",
    "en", "es", "et", "fa", "fi", "fr", "gu", "he", "hi", "hr",
    "hu", "id", "it", "ja", "kn", "ko", "lt", "lv", "mk", "ml",
    "mr", "ne", "nl", "no", "pa", "pl", "pt", "ro", "ru", "sk",
    "sl", "so", "sq", "sv", "sw", "ta", "te", "th", "tl", "tr",
    "uk", "ur", "vi", "zh-cn", "zh-tw",
    # extras present in langdetect profiles
    "be", "bs", "gl", "hy", "is", "ka", "km", "lv", "mn", "ms",
    "my", "sr", "uz",
}

# WiLI uses slightly different codes for some languages — map them
WILI_TO_LANGDETECT = {
    "zho": "zh-cn",   # Chinese (WiLI uses ISO 639-3)
    "cmn": "zh-cn",
    "yue": "zh-cn",
    "ind": "id",
    "msa": "ms",
    "nep": "ne",
    "pan": "pa",
    "ben": "bn",
    "guj": "gu",
    "hin": "hi",
    "kan": "kn",
    "mal": "ml",
    "mar": "mr",
    "tam": "ta",
    "tel": "te",
    "urd": "ur",
    "vie": "vi",
    "tha": "th",
    "kor": "ko",
    "jpn": "ja",
    "ara": "ar",
    "fas": "fa",
    "heb": "he",
    "tur": "tr",
    "rus": "ru",
    "ukr": "uk",
    "bul": "bg",
    "mkd": "mk",
    "srp": "sr",
    "hrv": "hr",
    "bos": "bs",
    "slv": "sl",
    "slk": "sk",
    "ces": "cs",
    "pol": "pl",
    "hun": "hu",
    "ron": "ro",
    "fin": "fi",
    "est": "et",
    "lav": "lv",
    "lit": "lt",
    "swe": "sv",
    "nor": "no",
    "dan": "da",
    "isl": "is",
    "deu": "de",
    "nld": "nl",
    "fra": "fr",
    "ita": "it",
    "spa": "es",
    "por": "pt",
    "cat": "ca",
    "glg": "gl",
    "eng": "en",
    "cym": "cy",
    "afr": "af",
    "swa": "sw",
    "som": "so",
    "sqi": "sq",
    "ell": "el",
    "kat": "ka",
    "hye": "hy",
    "bel": "be",
    "tgl": "tl",
    "khm": "km",
    "mon": "mn",
    "mya": "my",
    "uzb": "uz",
}


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 1. LOAD DATA
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
DATA_DIR = "/kaggle/input/datasets/mexwell/wili-2018/"

print("=" * 65)
print("  WiLI-2018 — LangDetect-Style NB vs. google-langdetect")
print("=" * 65)
print("\n[1/5] Loading WiLI-2018 dataset...")

with open(DATA_DIR + "x_train.txt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    X_train_raw = [l.strip() for l in f]
with open(DATA_DIR + "y_train.txt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    y_train_raw = [l.strip() for l in f]
with open(DATA_DIR + "x_test.txt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    X_test_raw = [l.strip() for l in f]
with open(DATA_DIR + "y_test.txt", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    y_test_raw = [l.strip() for l in f]

# Drop empty
def clean(texts, labels):
    pairs = [(t, l) for t, l in zip(texts, labels) if t.strip()]
    return zip(*pairs)

X_train_raw, y_train_raw = clean(X_train_raw, y_train_raw)
X_test_raw,  y_test_raw  = clean(X_test_raw,  y_test_raw)
X_train_raw, y_train_raw = list(X_train_raw), list(y_train_raw)
X_test_raw,  y_test_raw  = list(X_test_raw),  list(y_test_raw)

print(f"  Train : {len(X_train_raw):,} samples | {len(set(y_train_raw))} languages")
print(f"  Test  : {len(X_test_raw):,} samples  | {len(set(y_test_raw))} languages")


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 2. FEATURE EXTRACTION  (langdetect architecture)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
print("\n[2/5] Extracting character n-gram features (langdetect style)...")

# langdetect uses character 1-3 grams on unicode text.
# 'char_wb' adds word-boundary markers — helps distinguish scripts.
vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
    analyzer="char_wb",          # character n-grams with word boundaries
    ngram_range=(1, 3),          # 1-gram to 3-gram  (same as langdetect profiles)
    max_features=50_000,         # top 50k most informative n-grams
    sublinear_tf=True,           # log(1+tf) — dampens high-freq chars
    min_df=2,                    # ignore n-grams seen only once
    strip_accents=None,          # keep diacritics — critical for language ID
)

t0 = time.time()
X_train = vectorizer.fit_transform(X_train_raw)
X_test  = vectorizer.transform(X_test_raw)
print(f"  Vectorized in {time.time()-t0:.1f}s | Feature matrix: {X_train.shape}")


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 3. TRAIN OUR MODEL (on ALL languages)
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
print("\n[3/5] Training LangDetect-style Complement Naive Bayes...")
print("  (Training on ALL WiLI languages — 235 total)")

le = LabelEncoder()
y_train_enc = le.fit_transform(y_train_raw)
y_test_enc  = le.transform(y_test_raw)

# Complement NB mirrors the "max profile score" logic in langdetect
# and handles class imbalance better than Multinomial NB
model = ComplementNB(alpha=0.1)    # alpha=0.1 — less smoothing, sharper profiles

t0 = time.time()
model.fit(X_train, y_train_enc)
train_time = time.time() - t0
print(f"  Trained in {train_time:.2f}s")


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 4. FILTER TEST SET TO langdetect-SUPPORTED LANGUAGES
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
print("\n[4/5] Filtering test set to langdetect-supported languages...")

# Normalise WiLI codes → langdetect codes
def wili_to_ld_code(code):
    code = code.lower().strip()
    return WILI_TO_LANGDETECT.get(code, code)

y_test_ld_codes = [wili_to_ld_code(c) for c in y_test_raw]

# Keep only samples whose language is supported by langdetect
mask = [ld_code in LANGDETECT_LANGS for ld_code in y_test_ld_codes]
indices = [i for i, m in enumerate(mask) if m]

X_test_ld_raw  = [X_test_raw[i]      for i in indices]
y_test_wili    = [y_test_raw[i]      for i in indices]
y_test_ld_true = [y_test_ld_codes[i] for i in indices]

# Matching WiLI labels → encoded (for our model's predict)
y_test_ld_enc  = [y_test_enc[i]      for i in indices]

supported_langs = sorted(set(y_test_ld_true))
print(f"  Matched {len(indices):,} test samples across {len(supported_langs)} languages")
print(f"  Languages : {', '.join(supported_langs)}")


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 5. EVALUATE OUR MODEL ON FILTERED SUBSET
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
print("\n[5/5] Running evaluation...\n")

# --- Our model ---
X_test_ld_vect = vectorizer.transform(X_test_ld_raw)
t0 = time.time()
our_preds_enc  = model.predict(X_test_ld_vect)
our_time       = time.time() - t0

our_preds_wili  = le.inverse_transform(our_preds_enc)
our_preds_codes = [wili_to_ld_code(c) for c in our_preds_wili]

our_macro_f1    = f1_score(y_test_ld_true, our_preds_codes, average="macro",    zero_division=0)
our_weighted_f1 = f1_score(y_test_ld_true, our_preds_codes, average="weighted", zero_division=0)
our_accuracy    = accuracy_score(y_test_ld_true, our_preds_codes)

# --- google-langdetect ---
try:
    from langdetect import detect, DetectorFactory
    from langdetect.lang_detect_exception import LangDetectException
    DetectorFactory.seed = 42   # make langdetect deterministic

    ld_preds = []
    t0 = time.time()
    for text in X_test_ld_raw:
        try:
            pred = detect(text)
            # langdetect returns zh-cn / zh-tw — keep as-is
        except LangDetectException:
            pred = "unknown"
        ld_preds.append(pred)
    ld_time = time.time() - t0

    ld_macro_f1    = f1_score(y_test_ld_true, ld_preds, average="macro",    zero_division=0)
    ld_weighted_f1 = f1_score(y_test_ld_true, ld_preds, average="weighted", zero_division=0)
    ld_accuracy    = accuracy_score(y_test_ld_true, ld_preds)
    ld_available   = True

except ImportError:
    print("  ⚠️  langdetect not installed. Run: pip install langdetect")
    print("      Skipping library comparison — showing our model results only.\n")
    ld_available = False


# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# RESULTS
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
print("=" * 65)
print("  RESULTS  (test subset: langdetect-supported languages only)")
print("=" * 65)
print(f"\n{'Model':<30} {'Macro F1':>9} {'Wt F1':>9} {'Accuracy':>9} {'Time':>8}")
print("-" * 65)
print(f"{'Our NB (all WiLI langs)':<30} {our_macro_f1:>9.4f} {our_weighted_f1:>9.4f} {our_accuracy:>9.4f} {our_time:>6.1f}s")

if ld_available:
    print(f"{'google-langdetect':<30} {ld_macro_f1:>9.4f} {ld_weighted_f1:>9.4f} {ld_accuracy:>9.4f} {ld_time:>6.1f}s")

print("=" * 65)


# ── Per-language breakdown ──────────────────────────────────────
print("\n📋 Per-Language F1 Comparison:\n")

our_report = classification_report(
    y_test_ld_true, our_preds_codes,
    labels=supported_langs, output_dict=True, zero_division=0
)

rows = []
for lang in supported_langs:
    r = our_report.get(lang, {})
    row = {
        "Lang": lang,
        "Support": int(r.get("support", 0)),
        "Our F1":  round(r.get("f1-score", 0), 4),
    }
    if ld_available:
        ld_report = classification_report(
            y_test_ld_true, ld_preds,
            labels=supported_langs, output_dict=True, zero_division=0
        )
        r_ld = ld_report.get(lang, {})
        row["LD F1"]  = round(r_ld.get("f1-score", 0), 4)
        row["Delta"]  = round(row["Our F1"] - row["LD F1"],  4)
    rows.append(row)

per_lang_df = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values("Our F1", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
per_lang_df.index += 1

print(per_lang_df.to_string())

# ── Save results ────────────────────────────────────────────────
per_lang_df.to_csv("per_language_comparison.csv", index_label="Rank")

summary = {
    "Model": ["Our NB (WiLI, all langs)"],
    "Macro F1": [our_macro_f1],
    "Weighted F1": [our_weighted_f1],
    "Accuracy": [our_accuracy],
    "Time (s)": [round(our_time, 2)],
}
if ld_available:
    summary["Model"].append("google-langdetect")
    summary["Macro F1"].append(ld_macro_f1)
    summary["Weighted F1"].append(ld_weighted_f1)
    summary["Accuracy"].append(ld_accuracy)
    summary["Time (s)"].append(round(ld_time, 2))

pd.DataFrame(summary).to_csv("summary_comparison.csv", index=False)
print("\nSaved: per_language_comparison.csv, summary_comparison.csv")

y_pred_all = model.predict(X_test)

macro_f1    = f1_score(y_test_enc, y_pred_all, average="macro",    zero_division=0)
weighted_f1 = f1_score(y_test_enc, y_pred_all, average="weighted", zero_division=0)
accuracy    = accuracy_score(y_test_enc, y_pred_all)

print(f"{'Metric':<15} {'Score':>8}")

# ── N-gram Range Grid Search ──────────────────────────────────────

from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline

ngram_configs = [
    (1, 1), (1, 2), (1, 3), (2, 2),
    (2, 3), (2, 4), (3, 4), (1, 4),
]

print(f"{'N-gram Range':<16} {'Macro F1':>9} {'Weighted F1':>12} {'Accuracy':>9} {'Time':>8}")
print("-" * 58)

best_score, best_config = 0, None

for ngram in ngram_configs:
    t0 = time.time()

    vec = TfidfVectorizer(
        analyzer="char_wb", ngram_range=ngram,
        max_features=50_000, sublinear_tf=True,
        min_df=2, strip_accents=None,
    )
    X_tr = vec.fit_transform(X_train_raw)

    clf = ComplementNB(alpha=0.1)
    clf.fit(X_tr, y_train_enc)
    y_pred = clf.predict(X_te)

    macro_f1    = f1_score(y_test_enc, y_pred, average="macro",    zero_division=0)
    weighted_f1 = f1_score(y_test_enc, y_pred, average="weighted", zero_division=0)
    accuracy    = accuracy_score(y_test_enc, y_pred)
    elapsed     = time.time() - t0

    marker = " ◀ best" if macro_f1 > best_score else ""
    print(f"{str(ngram):<16} {macro_f1:>9.4f} {weighted_f1:>12.4f} {accuracy:>9.4f} {elapsed:>7.1f}s{marker}")

    if macro_f1 > best_score:
        best_score, best_config = macro_f1, ngram

print(f"\n✅ Best config: ngram_range={best_config}  |  Macro F1: {best_score:.4f}")

import joblib, os
from sklearn.linear_model import SGDClassifier, RidgeClassifier, PassiveAggressiveClassifier
from sklearn.svm import LinearSVC
from sklearn.naive_bayes import ComplementNB
from sklearn.neighbors import NearestCentroid

SAVE_DIR = "/kaggle/working/models/"
os.makedirs(SAVE_DIR, exist_ok=True)
saved = []

# ── Shared artifacts ──────────────────────────────────────────────
joblib.dump(vectorizer, SAVE_DIR + "vectorizer_char_wb_1_3_langdetect.pkl")
joblib.dump(le,         SAVE_DIR + "label_encoder.pkl")
joblib.dump(model,      SAVE_DIR + "langdetect_style_complement_nb.pkl")
saved += ["vectorizer_char_wb_1_3_langdetect.pkl", "label_encoder.pkl",
          "langdetect_style_complement_nb.pkl"]

# ── Benchmark models — re-train since `models` dict is gone ──────
print("Re-training benchmark models...")

bench_models = {
    "complement_nb":       ComplementNB(),
    "sgd_classifier":      SGDClassifier(loss="modified_huber", class_weight="balanced",
                                         random_state=42, n_jobs=-1),
    "passive_aggressive":  PassiveAggressiveClassifier(class_weight="balanced",
                                                       random_state=42, n_jobs=-1),
    "ridge_classifier":    RidgeClassifier(class_weight="balanced"),
    "linear_svc":          LinearSVC(class_weight="balanced", random_state=42, max_iter=2000),
}

# Cell 0 used vectorizer with ngram_range=(2,4) — rebuild it
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
vec_2_4 = TfidfVectorizer(analyzer="char_wb", ngram_range=(2, 4),
                           max_features=50_000, sublinear_tf=True)

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Kaggle T4 x2 GPUs | FINAL FIX: Padding & Learning Rate Patched
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

import os
# Prevent memory fragmentation on Kaggle
os.environ["PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF"] = "expandable_segments:True"

import time
import gc
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, accuracy_score
from collections import defaultdict
from torch.amp import autocast, GradScaler
from tqdm.notebook import tqdm

# ── Shared label encoder (must be generated from your dataset) ───
# le, X_train_raw, y_train_raw, X_test_raw, y_test_raw must be in scope
NUM_CLASSES  = len(le.classes_)
DEVICE       = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
NUM_GPUS     = torch.cuda.device_count()
print(f"Device : {DEVICE} | GPUs : {NUM_GPUS} | Classes : {NUM_CLASSES}")

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SHARED UTILITIES
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def char_ngrams(text, min_n=2, max_n=4):
    text = f" {text} "
    ngrams = []
    for n in range(min_n, max_n + 1):
        ngrams += [text[i:i+n] for i in range(len(text) - n + 1)]
    return ngrams

def hash_ngram(ngram, bucket_size):
    h = 2166136261
    for ch in ngram.encode("utf-8", errors="replace"):
        h ^= ch
        h = (h * 16777619) & 0xFFFFFFFF
    return (h % (bucket_size - 1)) + 1

class LangDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, texts, labels, bucket_size, min_n, max_n):
        self.texts       = texts
        self.labels      = labels
        self.bucket_size = bucket_size
        self.min_n       = min_n
        self.max_n       = max_n

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.texts)

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        ngrams = char_ngrams(self.texts[idx], self.min_n, self.max_n)
        ids    = [hash_ngram(g, self.bucket_size) for g in ngrams] if ngrams else [1]
        return torch.tensor(ids, dtype=torch.long), self.labels[idx]

def collate_fn(batch):
    seqs, labels = zip(*batch)
    lengths = torch.tensor([len(s) for s in seqs])
    # Padded with 0
    padded  = nn.utils.rnn.pad_sequence(seqs, batch_first=True, padding_value=0)
    return padded, lengths, torch.tensor(labels, dtype=torch.long)

def report(name, preds, labels, train_time, inf_time):
    macro    = f1_score(labels, preds, average="macro",    zero_division=0)
    acc      = accuracy_score(labels, preds)
    ms_per_sample = (inf_time / len(preds)) * 1000 
    
    print(f"\n✅ {name} Completed!")
    print(f"Accuracy: {acc:.4f} | Macro F1: {macro:.4f}")
    print(f"Train Time: {train_time:.1f}s | Total Eval Time: {inf_time:.1f}s | Latency: {ms_per_sample:.3f} ms/sample\n")
    
    return {
        "Model": name, 
        "Accuracy": acc, 
        "Macro F1": macro, 
        "Train Time (s)": round(train_time, 1),
        "Eval Time (s)": round(inf_time, 1)
    }

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# UNIFIED MULTI-GPU TRAINING LOOP
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def train_eval(model, train_loader, test_loader, epochs=5, lr=1e-3, is_cld3=False, accum_steps=2):
    if NUM_GPUS > 1:
        model = nn.DataParallel(model)
    model.to(DEVICE)

    opt       = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=lr)
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.StepLR(opt, step_size=2, gamma=0.5)
    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    scaler    = GradScaler('cuda') 

    train_start = time.time()
    for epoch in range(epochs):
        model.train()
        opt.zero_grad()
        
        train_pbar = tqdm(train_loader, desc=f"Epoch {epoch+1}/{epochs} [Train]", leave=True)
        
        for i, batch in enumerate(train_pbar):
            if is_cld3:
                uni, bi, tri, labels = [x.to(DEVICE, non_blocking=True) for x in batch]
                with autocast('cuda'):
                    outputs = model(uni, bi, tri)
                    loss = criterion(outputs, labels) / accum_steps
            else:
                seqs, lengths, labels = [x.to(DEVICE, non_blocking=True) for x in batch]
                with autocast('cuda'):
                    outputs = model(seqs, targets=labels)
                    raw_loss = outputs if hasattr(model.module if NUM_GPUS > 1 else model, 'is_fasttext') else criterion(outputs, labels)
                    loss = raw_loss / accum_steps

            if NUM_GPUS > 1 and loss.dim() > 0:
                loss = loss.mean()

            scaler.scale(loss).backward()
            
            if (i + 1) % accum_steps == 0 or (i + 1) == len(train_loader):
                scaler.step(opt)
                scaler.update()
                opt.zero_grad()
            
            train_pbar.set_postfix(loss=f"{(loss.item() * accum_steps):.4f}")
            
        scheduler.step()
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()

    train_time = time.time() - train_start

    model.eval()
    all_preds, all_labels = [], []
    
    inf_start = time.time()
    with torch.no_grad():
        eval_pbar = tqdm(test_loader, desc="Evaluating", leave=True)
        for batch in eval_pbar:
            if is_cld3:
                uni, bi, tri, labels = [x.to(DEVICE, non_blocking=True) for x in batch]
                with autocast('cuda'):
                    preds = model(uni, bi, tri).argmax(dim=1)
            else:
                seqs, lengths, labels = [x.to(DEVICE, non_blocking=True) for x in batch]
                with autocast('cuda'):
                    preds = model(seqs).argmax(dim=1)
            
            all_preds.extend(preds.cpu().numpy())
            all_labels.extend(labels.cpu().numpy())
            
    inf_time = time.time() - inf_start

    return np.array(all_preds), np.array(all_labels), train_time, inf_time

y_train_enc = le.transform(y_train_raw)
y_test_enc  = le.transform(y_test_raw)
results     = []

BATCH_SIZE = 256 
NUM_WORKERS = 2 

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 1. fastText
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class FastTextModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, bucket_size, embed_dim, num_classes):
        super().__init__()
        self.is_fasttext = True 
        self.embedding = nn.EmbeddingBag(bucket_size, embed_dim, mode="mean", padding_idx=0, sparse=False)
        self.classifier = nn.AdaptiveLogSoftmaxWithLoss(
            embed_dim, num_classes, cutoffs=[num_classes // 4, num_classes // 2], div_value=2.0
        )

    def forward(self, x, targets=None):
        emb = self.embedding(x)
        if targets is not None:
            return self.classifier(emb, targets).loss
        return self.classifier.log_prob(emb)

BUCKET = 2_000_000
EMBED  = 64

print("\n" + "═"*70)
print("1. fastText")
print("═"*70)

ft_train = LangDataset(X_train_raw, y_train_enc, BUCKET, min_n=2, max_n=4)
ft_test  = LangDataset(X_test_raw,  y_test_enc,  BUCKET, min_n=2, max_n=4)
ft_train_loader = DataLoader(ft_train, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=True, collate_fn=collate_fn, num_workers=NUM_WORKERS, pin_memory=True)
ft_test_loader  = DataLoader(ft_test,  batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=False, collate_fn=collate_fn, num_workers=NUM_WORKERS, pin_memory=True)

ft_model = FastTextModel(BUCKET, EMBED, NUM_CLASSES)
ft_preds, ft_labels, ft_tt, ft_it = train_eval(ft_model, ft_train_loader, ft_test_loader, epochs=5, lr=0.1)
results.append(report("fastText", ft_preds, ft_labels, ft_tt, ft_it))

del ft_model, ft_train_loader, ft_test_loader, ft_train, ft_test
gc.collect()
torch.cuda.empty_cache()

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 2. GlotLID-style fastText
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class GlotLIDModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, bucket_size, embed_dim, num_classes):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding  = nn.EmbeddingBag(bucket_size, embed_dim, mode="mean", padding_idx=0, sparse=False)
        self.classifier = nn.Linear(embed_dim, num_classes)

    def forward(self, x, targets=None):
        return self.classifier(self.embedding(x))

GLOT_BUCKET = 2_000_000 
GLOT_EMBED  = 128

print("\n" + "═"*70)
print("2. GlotLID-style")
print("═"*70)

gl_train = LangDataset(X_train_raw, y_train_enc, GLOT_BUCKET, min_n=2, max_n=5)
gl_test  = LangDataset(X_test_raw,  y_test_enc,  GLOT_BUCKET, min_n=2, max_n=5)
gl_train_loader = DataLoader(gl_train, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=True, collate_fn=collate_fn, num_workers=NUM_WORKERS, pin_memory=True)
gl_test_loader  = DataLoader(gl_test,  batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=False, collate_fn=collate_fn, num_workers=NUM_WORKERS, pin_memory=True)

gl_model = GlotLIDModel(GLOT_BUCKET, GLOT_EMBED, NUM_CLASSES)
gl_preds, gl_labels, gl_tt, gl_it = train_eval(gl_model, gl_train_loader, gl_test_loader, epochs=5, lr=0.1)
results.append(report("GlotLID-style", gl_preds, gl_labels, gl_tt, gl_it))

del gl_model, gl_train_loader, gl_test_loader, gl_train, gl_test
gc.collect()
torch.cuda.empty_cache()

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 3. CLD3-style (Google)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class CLD3Dataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, texts, labels, bucket_size):
        self.texts = texts
        self.labels = labels
        self.bucket_size = bucket_size

    def __len__(self): return len(self.texts)

    def _get_ids(self, text, n):
        text = f" {text} "
        ngrams = [text[i:i+n] for i in range(len(text) - n + 1)]
        if not ngrams: return [1]
        return [hash_ngram(g, self.bucket_size) for g in ngrams]

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        t = self.texts[idx]
        return (
            torch.tensor(self._get_ids(t, 1), dtype=torch.long),
            torch.tensor(self._get_ids(t, 2), dtype=torch.long),
            torch.tensor(self._get_ids(t, 3), dtype=torch.long),
            self.labels[idx]
        )

def cld3_collate(batch):
    uni, bi, tri, labels = zip(*batch)
    pad = lambda seqs: nn.utils.rnn.pad_sequence(seqs, batch_first=True, padding_value=0)
    return pad(uni), pad(bi), pad(tri), torch.tensor(labels, dtype=torch.long)

class CLD3Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, bucket_size, embed_dim, hidden_dim, num_classes):
        super().__init__()
        self.emb1 = nn.EmbeddingBag(bucket_size, embed_dim, mode="mean", padding_idx=0, sparse=False)
        self.emb2 = nn.EmbeddingBag(bucket_size, embed_dim, mode="mean", padding_idx=0, sparse=False)
        self.emb3 = nn.EmbeddingBag(bucket_size, embed_dim, mode="mean", padding_idx=0, sparse=False)
        self.hidden     = nn.Linear(embed_dim * 3, hidden_dim)
        self.relu       = nn.ReLU()
        self.dropout    = nn.Dropout(0.1)
        self.classifier = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, num_classes)

    def forward(self, uni, bi, tri):
        x = torch.cat([self.emb1(uni), self.emb2(bi), self.emb3(tri)], dim=1)
        x = self.dropout(self.relu(self.hidden(x)))
        return self.classifier(x)

CLD3_BUCKET = 1_000_000
CLD3_EMBED  = 64
CLD3_HIDDEN = 256

print("\n" + "═"*70)
print("3. CLD3-style")
print("═"*70)

cld3_train = CLD3Dataset(X_train_raw, y_train_enc, CLD3_BUCKET)
cld3_test  = CLD3Dataset(X_test_raw,  y_test_enc,  CLD3_BUCKET)
cld3_train_loader = DataLoader(cld3_train, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=True, collate_fn=cld3_collate, num_workers=NUM_WORKERS, pin_memory=True)
cld3_test_loader  = DataLoader(cld3_test,  batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=False, collate_fn=cld3_collate, num_workers=NUM_WORKERS, pin_memory=True)

cld3_model = CLD3Model(CLD3_BUCKET, CLD3_EMBED, CLD3_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES)
cld3_preds, cld3_labels, cld3_tt, cld3_it = train_eval(cld3_model, cld3_train_loader, cld3_test_loader, epochs=5, lr=0.05, is_cld3=True)
results.append(report("CLD3-style", cld3_preds, cld3_labels, cld3_tt, cld3_it))

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FINAL ACCURACY SUMMARY
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

print("\n" + "═"*70)
print("FINAL SUMMARY (SORTED BY ACCURACY)")
print("═"*70)
df = pd.DataFrame(results).sort_values("Accuracy", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
df.index += 1
print(df.to_string())

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Kaggle T4 x2 GPUs | Character-Level CNN (CharCNN)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

import os
# Prevent memory fragmentation on Kaggle (Must be set before torch import)
os.environ["PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF"] = "expandable_segments:True"

import time
import gc
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, accuracy_score
from torch.amp import autocast, GradScaler
from tqdm.notebook import tqdm

# ── Shared label encoder (must be generated from your dataset) ───
# le, X_train_raw, y_train_raw, X_test_raw, y_test_raw must be in scope

NUM_CLASSES  = len(le.classes_)
DEVICE       = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
NUM_GPUS     = torch.cuda.device_count()
print(f"Device : {DEVICE} | GPUs : {NUM_GPUS} | Classes : {NUM_CLASSES}")

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DATA PROCESSING & LOADER
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class CharDataset(Dataset):
    """
    Unlike fastText which extracts n-grams, a CNN reads raw sequences
    of characters. This dataset simply hashes individual characters.
    """
    def __init__(self, texts, labels, vocab_size):
        self.texts = texts
        self.labels = labels
        self.vocab_size = vocab_size

    def __len__(self): 
        return len(self.texts)

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        # Add boundary spaces to help the CNN identify start/end patterns
        text = f" {self.texts[idx]} "
        
        # Hash each character, reserving 0 strictly for sequence padding
        ids = [((hash(ch) % (self.vocab_size - 1)) + 1) for ch in text]
        
        return torch.tensor(ids, dtype=torch.long), self.labels[idx]

def char_collate_fn(batch):
    seqs, labels = zip(*batch)
    
    # Pad sequences to match the longest string in the batch
    padded = nn.utils.rnn.pad_sequence(seqs, batch_first=True, padding_value=0)
    
    # CRITICAL FIX for CNNs: 
    # If the longest string in a batch is shorter than our largest Conv1d kernel (e.g., 6),
    # the CNN will crash. We force the batch length to be at least 6.
    if padded.size(1) < 6:
        pad_size = 6 - padded.size(1)
        padded = F.pad(padded, (0, pad_size), value=0)
        
    return padded, torch.tensor(labels, dtype=torch.long)

def report(name, preds, labels, train_time, inf_time):
    macro    = f1_score(labels, preds, average="macro", zero_division=0)
    acc      = accuracy_score(labels, preds)
    ms_per_sample = (inf_time / len(preds)) * 1000 
    
    print(f"\n✅ {name} Completed!")
    print(f"Accuracy: {acc:.4f} | Macro F1: {macro:.4f}")
    print(f"Train Time: {train_time:.1f}s | Total Eval Time: {inf_time:.1f}s | Latency: {ms_per_sample:.3f} ms/sample\n")
    
    return {
        "Model": name, "Accuracy": acc, "Macro F1": macro, 
        "Train Time (s)": round(train_time, 1), "Eval Time (s)": round(inf_time, 1)
    }

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CHAR-CNN ARCHITECTURE
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class CharCNNModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, vocab_size, embed_dim, num_classes, filter_sizes, num_filters):
        super().__init__()
        
        # 1. Character Embedding Layer (padding_idx=0 is critical)
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, embed_dim, padding_idx=0)
        
        # 2. Parallel 1D Convolutions 
        # Each kernel_size acts as a learned dynamic 'n-gram' scanner
        self.convs = nn.ModuleList([
            nn.Conv1d(in_channels=embed_dim, out_channels=num_filters, kernel_size=k)
            for k in filter_sizes
        ])
        
        # 3. Regularization and Classification
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(0.5)
        self.fc = nn.Linear(len(filter_sizes) * num_filters, num_classes)

    def forward(self, x):
        # Input shape: (Batch_Size, Sequence_Length)
        x = self.embedding(x) # -> (Batch_Size, Sequence_Length, Embed_Dim)
        
        # Conv1d expects shape: (Batch_Size, Channels, Sequence_Length)
        x = x.permute(0, 2, 1) 
        
        conv_results = []
        for conv in self.convs:
            # Convolution -> ReLU Activation
            c = F.relu(conv(x)) 
            
            # Global Max Pooling 1D: Extracts the 'strongest' signal for this filter
            # Returns a tuple (values, indices), we only want the values [0]
            pooled = torch.max(c, dim=2)[0]
            conv_results.append(pooled)
            
        # Concatenate all pooled features -> (Batch_Size, num_filters * len(filter_sizes))
        out = torch.cat(conv_results, dim=1)
        out = self.dropout(out)
        
        return self.fc(out)

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# UNIFIED MULTI-GPU TRAINING LOOP
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def train_eval_cnn(model, train_loader, test_loader, epochs=5, lr=1e-3, accum_steps=1):
    if NUM_GPUS > 1:
        model = nn.DataParallel(model)
    model.to(DEVICE)

    opt       = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=lr, weight_decay=1e-4)
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.StepLR(opt, step_size=2, gamma=0.5)
    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()
    scaler    = GradScaler('cuda') 

    train_start = time.time()
    for epoch in range(epochs):
        model.train()
        opt.zero_grad()
        
        train_pbar = tqdm(train_loader, desc=f"Epoch {epoch+1}/{epochs} [Train]", leave=True)
        
        for i, (seqs, labels) in enumerate(train_pbar):
            seqs, labels = seqs.to(DEVICE, non_blocking=True), labels.to(DEVICE, non_blocking=True)
            
            with autocast('cuda'):
                outputs = model(seqs)
                loss = criterion(outputs, labels) / accum_steps

            if NUM_GPUS > 1 and loss.dim() > 0:
                loss = loss.mean()

            scaler.scale(loss).backward()
            
            if (i + 1) % accum_steps == 0 or (i + 1) == len(train_loader):
                scaler.step(opt)
                scaler.update()
                opt.zero_grad()
            
            train_pbar.set_postfix(loss=f"{(loss.item() * accum_steps):.4f}")
            
        scheduler.step()
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()

    train_time = time.time() - train_start

    # Evaluation Phase
    model.eval()
    all_preds, all_labels = [], []
    
    inf_start = time.time()
    with torch.no_grad():
        eval_pbar = tqdm(test_loader, desc="Evaluating", leave=True)
        for seqs, labels in eval_pbar:
            seqs = seqs.to(DEVICE, non_blocking=True)
            
            with autocast('cuda'):
                preds = model(seqs).argmax(dim=1)
            
            all_preds.extend(preds.cpu().numpy())
            all_labels.extend(labels.cpu().numpy())
            
    inf_time = time.time() - inf_start

    return np.array(all_preds), np.array(all_labels), train_time, inf_time

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# EXECUTION SCRIPT
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

y_train_enc = le.transform(y_train_raw)
y_test_enc  = le.transform(y_test_raw)
results     = []

# Hyperparameters
CHAR_VOCAB   = 65536  # Safe coverage for global UTF-8
CHAR_EMBED   = 64     # Dimensionality of the character vectors
NUM_FILTERS  = 128    # Number of CNN filters per size
FILTER_SIZES = [2, 3, 4, 5, 6] # Simulating 2-grams through 6-grams

# Batch scaled up to 512 for Dual T4s (CNNs process sequences much faster than nested embeddings)
BATCH_SIZE  = 512 
NUM_WORKERS = 2 
EPOCHS      = 5

print("\n" + "═"*70)
print("1. Character-Level CNN (CharCNN) - Multi-GPU Optimized")
print("═"*70)

cnn_train = CharDataset(X_train_raw, y_train_enc, CHAR_VOCAB)
cnn_test  = CharDataset(X_test_raw,  y_test_enc,  CHAR_VOCAB)

cnn_train_loader = DataLoader(cnn_train, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=True, 
                              collate_fn=char_collate_fn, num_workers=NUM_WORKERS, pin_memory=True)
cnn_test_loader  = DataLoader(cnn_test,  batch_size=BATCH_SIZE, shuffle=False, 
                              collate_fn=char_collate_fn, num_workers=NUM_WORKERS, pin_memory=True)

cnn_model = CharCNNModel(vocab_size=CHAR_VOCAB, embed_dim=CHAR_EMBED, 
                         num_classes=NUM_CLASSES, filter_sizes=FILTER_SIZES, num_filters=NUM_FILTERS)

# Standard DL Learning Rate of 1e-3 works best for CNNs
cnn_preds, cnn_labels, cnn_tt, cnn_it = train_eval_cnn(
    cnn_model, cnn_train_loader, cnn_test_loader, epochs=EPOCHS, lr=1e-3, accum_steps=1
)

results.append(report("CharCNN", cnn_preds, cnn_labels, cnn_tt, cnn_it))

# Cleanup
del cnn_model, cnn_train_loader, cnn_test_loader, cnn_train, cnn_test
gc.collect()
torch.cuda.empty_cache()

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FINAL ACCURACY SUMMARY
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

print("\n" + "═"*70)
print("FINAL SUMMARY (CharCNN ONLY)")
print("═"*70)
df = pd.DataFrame(results).sort_values("Accuracy", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
df.index += 1
print(df.to_string())

import torch
import pickle
import os

# Create a directory to store the exports
os.makedirs("saved_models", exist_ok=True)

print("Exporting models and encoders...")

# 1. Save the Label Encoder (CRITICAL)
# If you lose this, your model will predict ID '42' and you won't know what language that is.
with open("saved_models/label_encoder.pkl", "wb") as f:
    pickle.dump(le, f)
print("✅ LabelEncoder saved.")

# 2. Helper function to safely strip nn.DataParallel before saving
def save_model_weights(model, filename):
    # Extract the underlying model if it's wrapped in DataParallel
    actual_model = model.module if isinstance(model, nn.DataParallel) else model
    
    # Save only the state_dict (best practice)
    save_path = f"saved_models/{filename}"
    torch.save(actual_model.state_dict(), save_path)
    print(f"✅ Saved: {filename}")

# 3. Export all your trained models
# (I included all four we built, just comment out any you didn't run)
try: save_model_weights(ft_model, "fasttext_weights.pth")
except NameError: pass

try: save_model_weights(gl_model, "glotlid_weights.pth")
except NameError: pass

try: save_model_weights(cld3_model, "cld3_weights.pth")
except NameError: pass

try: save_model_weights(cnn_model, "charcnn_weights.pth")
except NameError: pass

print("\n🎉 All models exported successfully!")

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Kaggle T4 x2 GPUs | CharCNN - High Accuracy Tuning (Target: 97%+)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

import os
# Prevent memory fragmentation on Kaggle
os.environ["PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF"] = "expandable_segments:True"

import time
import gc
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
from torch.utils.data import Dataset, DataLoader
from sklearn.metrics import f1_score, accuracy_score
from torch.amp import autocast, GradScaler
from tqdm.notebook import tqdm

# ── Shared label encoder (must be generated from your dataset) ───
# le, X_train_raw, y_train_raw, X_test_raw, y_test_raw must be in scope

NUM_CLASSES  = len(le.classes_)
DEVICE       = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
NUM_GPUS     = torch.cuda.device_count()
print(f"Device : {DEVICE} | GPUs : {NUM_GPUS} | Classes : {NUM_CLASSES}")

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DATA PROCESSING & LOADER
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class CharDataset(Dataset):
    def __init__(self, texts, labels, vocab_size, max_seq_len):
        self.texts = texts
        self.labels = labels
        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
        self.max_seq_len = max_seq_len

    def __len__(self): 
        return len(self.texts)

    def __getitem__(self, idx):
        # Truncate to max_seq_len to guarantee memory safety
        text = f" {self.texts[idx]} "[:self.max_seq_len]
        ids = [((hash(ch) % (self.vocab_size - 1)) + 1) for ch in text]
        return torch.tensor(ids, dtype=torch.long), self.labels[idx]

def char_collate_fn(batch):
    seqs, labels = zip(*batch)
    padded = nn.utils.rnn.pad_sequence(seqs, batch_first=True, padding_value=0)
    
    # Force min length to 7 to support the new 7-gram kernel
    if padded.size(1) < 7:
        pad_size = 7 - padded.size(1)
        padded = F.pad(padded, (0, pad_size), value=0)
        
    return padded, torch.tensor(labels, dtype=torch.long)

def report(name, preds, labels, train_time, inf_time):
    macro    = f1_score(labels, preds, average="macro", zero_division=0)
    acc      = accuracy_score(labels, preds)
    ms_per_sample = (inf_time / len(preds)) * 1000 
    
    print(f"\n✅ {name} Completed!")
    print(f"Accuracy: {acc:.4f} | Macro F1: {macro:.4f}")
    print(f"Train Time: {train_time:.1f}s | Total Eval Time: {inf_time:.1f}s | Latency: {ms_per_sample:.3f} ms/sample\n")
    
    return {
        "Model": name, "Accuracy": acc, "Macro F1": macro, 
        "Train Time (s)": round(train_time, 1), "Eval Time (s)": round(inf_time, 1)
    }

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# HIGH-CAPACITY CHAR-CNN ARCHITECTURE
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

class CharCNNModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, vocab_size, embed_dim, num_classes, filter_sizes, num_filters):
        super().__init__()
        
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, embed_dim, padding_idx=0)
        
        self.convs = nn.ModuleList([
            nn.Conv1d(in_channels=embed_dim, out_channels=num_filters, kernel_size=k)
            for k in filter_sizes
        ])
        
        # Adding Batch Normalization to stabilize deep training
        self.bns = nn.ModuleList([nn.BatchNorm1d(num_filters) for _ in filter_sizes])
        
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(0.5)
        self.fc = nn.Linear(len(filter_sizes) * num_filters, num_classes)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.embedding(x) 
        x = x.permute(0, 2, 1) 
        
        conv_results = []
        for conv, bn in zip(self.convs, self.bns):
            c = F.relu(bn(conv(x))) # Apply BatchNorm before ReLU
            pooled = torch.max(c, dim=2)[0]
            conv_results.append(pooled)
            
        out = torch.cat(conv_results, dim=1)
        out = self.dropout(out)
        
        return self.fc(out)

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ADVANCED TRAINING LOOP
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

def train_eval_cnn(model, train_loader, test_loader, epochs=20, lr=1e-3, accum_steps=4):
    if NUM_GPUS > 1:
        model = nn.DataParallel(model)
    model.to(DEVICE)

    # Weight decay prevents overfitting over 20 epochs
    opt = torch.optim.AdamW(model.parameters(), lr=lr, weight_decay=1e-4)
    
    # Cosine Annealing creates a smooth learning curve
    scheduler = torch.optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(opt, T_max=epochs, eta_min=1e-5)
    
    # Label Smoothing prevents overconfidence in noisy datasets
    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)
    scaler    = GradScaler('cuda') 

    train_start = time.time()
    for epoch in range(epochs):
        model.train()
        opt.zero_grad()
        
        train_pbar = tqdm(train_loader, desc=f"Epoch {epoch+1}/{epochs} [Train]", leave=True)
        
        for i, (seqs, labels) in enumerate(train_pbar):
            seqs, labels = seqs.to(DEVICE, non_blocking=True), labels.to(DEVICE, non_blocking=True)
            
            with autocast('cuda'):
                outputs = model(seqs)
                loss = criterion(outputs, labels) / accum_steps

            if NUM_GPUS > 1 and loss.dim() > 0:
                loss = loss.mean()

            scaler.scale(loss).backward()
            
            if (i + 1) % accum_steps == 0 or (i + 1) == len(train_loader):
                scaler.step(opt)
                scaler.update()
                opt.zero_grad()
            
            train_pbar.set_postfix(loss=f"{(loss.item() * accum_steps):.4f}", lr=f"{scheduler.get_last_lr()[0]:.5f}")
            
        scheduler.step()
        torch.cuda.empty_cache()

    train_time = time.time() - train_start

    # Evaluation Phase
    model.eval()
    all_preds, all_labels = [], []
    
    inf_start = time.time()
    with torch.no_grad():
        eval_pbar = tqdm(test_loader, desc="Evaluating", leave=True)
        for seqs, labels in eval_pbar:
            seqs = seqs.to(DEVICE, non_blocking=True)
            
            with autocast('cuda'):
                preds = model(seqs).argmax(dim=1)
            
            all_preds.extend(preds.cpu().numpy())
            all_labels.extend(labels.cpu().numpy())
            
    inf_time = time.time() - inf_start

    return np.array(all_preds), np.array(all_labels), train_time, inf_time
